1213222111111121111331121111111112111123.content:
5 weeks seemed to go by in the blink of an eye as Winter Break was now over and school was back in session. Even though it was still winter, you wouldn't be able to tell since it was still sunny and warm out. Such is life on an island near the equator, summer like weather nearly all year around.

We now find our young hero Syrus in the middle of his toughest challenge yet, surviving Tennis training with the schools Tennis Captain, Harrington. Syrus got into this mess by nearly hitting Alexis with a Tennis ball he knocked way out of bounds, only for Harrington to swat it away in time, and hit Dr. Crowler right in the eye. And so Crowler, looking for any excuse to give any Slifer a hard time, (even if said Slifer was the Top Duelist in school) decided to have Harrington "whip Syrus into shape" as punishment for hitting him in the eye.

Completely ignoring the fact that Harrington was the one who actually hit him. After all, he was a 3rd year Obelisk Blue who came from a rich family, he didn't deserve to be punished.

"Huff..huff..Is this guy..huff..trying to..huff..kill me..?" Syrus panted in exhaustion as he laid on the floor surrounded by tennis balls.

Syrus may be a top notch duelist, but an athlete he was not. Not by a long shot. The only thing he had going for him was his new enhanced stamina thanks to his victory against Jinzo. But even that could get him so far against a guy who was both a natural born athlete and been training in sports nearly all his life, like Harrington.

"Come on Syrus no pain no gain." Harrington stated still rearing to go for more "You've got to hustle to build that muscle. You've got to sweat to become a threat. If you don't pick up the pace you won't win the race."

"Harrington, you can quit it with the sports cliches! I get it alright!" Syrus exclaimed in annoyance. He was really starting to not like this guy. Not only was he a complete fitness nut, but he seemed to enjoy the fact that he had the freedom to boss Syrus around with his crazy practicing.

Plus he tried to put the moves on Alexis earlier! Unsuccessfully mind you, but still!

"Hey, there is no letter I, in the word team Syrus!" Harrington continued "Why that's the very first rule in Tennis!"

"Really?" Syrus asked confused "Even when your playing Singles?"

"Moving on!" Harrington ignored his fair point "It's time we got to work on your Forehand and Backhand! I think a thousand strokes will make Tennis more your racket! Get it?!" he asked like he made the best joke ever.

All Syrus did was groan at the painful pun.

Meanwhile Jaden, Bastion, Jazz and Mindy watched on with varying degrees of dismay for Syrus's situation.

"I get it. This guy is nuts!" Jaden stated strongly "The cliches! This crazy practicing! And worst of all is that Syrus has to put up with it because Crowler won't punish the guy cause he's an Obelisk!"

"Indeed. It is most unfair." Bastion agreed as he held a bottle of water for Syrus for when he needed it. "Unfortunately there's not much we can do about it." He then notice that Jazz and Mindy looked particularly uneasy about all this "Is everything alright ladies?"

Jazz and Mindy just gave a shared shaky smile and said at the same time "Yeah, we're fine."

They then looked back at their struggling lover with worry and Harrington with disgust. What was disturbing them so much is that not long ago, Harrington was the sort of guy they would be head over heels for. Tall, athletic, handsome, rich, a killer smile full of perfect teeth. But also obsessive, full of himself and clearly a bit of a bully with the way he was taking pleasure in "whipping Syrus into shape."

[I can't believe that I used to think that this was the kind of guy I would want to set Alexis up with or date myself.] Jazz thought to herself with self-disgust.

Mindy wasn't fairing much better as she rubbed her arms while a repulsed shiver crawled through her skin [I feel unclean.] she lamented to herself.

A few minutes later and Syrus was once again on the floor panting like an overheated dog after completing the first hundred strokes. Harrington had to admit he was a little impressed with the short duelist's endurance, even he was really starting to sweat and get short of breath. "Alright, take five!" he called out, causing Syrus to sigh in deep relief.

"Syrus! Incoming!" Bastion called out as he tossed the water bottle to his friend.

Syrus caught it with both hands "Thanks Bastion!" he said gratefully as he guzzled down the cool liquid.

Mindy then spotted from the corner of her eye that Alexis had walked into the court "Hi Alexis!" she greeted friendly.

Hearing that the Number 1 Babe in Duel Academy was on the court had Harrington turning on his charm to maximum "Alexis?" he greeted with his best smile, complete with a brief sparkle.

Alexis was unimpressed. She dealt with guys like him her whole life and she didn't have time for it.

But Harrington didn't pick up on that as he ran up to her excitedly "Hey! Sorry I'm sweating so much! I've just been spending the whole afternoon whipping this kid into-" he paused and gasped in disbelief when he noticed that she completely ghosted him and walked over to where Syrus was sitting.

"Syrus, you won't believe what I just heard." Alexis stated like she had big news.

"Huh?" he asked as he placed his now empty water bottle on the floor and got up to talk to his girlfriend properly.

"I was on my way here when I ran into Prof. Banner. He told me someone spotted Chazz!" she explained.

"Really?!" Syrus asked surprised, as nobody had heard about what happen to the wayward duelist in almost 2 months "Well where is he?"

Meanwhile Harrington watched the scene with an aura of vengeful fire surrounding him. Seeing a dorky little wimp like Syrus talking to a goddess like Alexis was the final straw for him. Ever since that puny dweeb had somehow beaten Zane, the social order of Duel Academy had been turn on it's head. All the Slifers grew backbones and began to stand up for themselves whenever someone of higher rank tried to put them in their place. And most of them succeeded! Worst still, is that he even seen some of his Obelisk peers actually starting to treat the Slifer scum like equals! Even friends!

And all because some nobody got lucky against the Top Dog on campus! Well No More he said! It was time to start putting things back to normal by putting the runt in his place once and for all!

"HEY!!! TIME OUT!!!" Harrington hollered as he approached the secret couple with aura still burning hot "You can't talk to a first round pick like Alexis!! I mean, you can't even return a Buggy-Whip with some Top-Spin!! So stay away from my little Obelisk Pixie!!"

"Obelisk Pixie???" asked a very confused Jaden to Bastion to see if he ever heard of such a thing. The Ra genius gave a shrug to show he was just as puzzled.

"Listen Syrus, I'm warning you!! Just step away from the beautiful girl!!" Harrington exclaimed as his eyes seemed to glow a demonic red "You're way out of your league!! Do not make me go athletic on you!!"

Syrus wasn't intimidated in the slightest. Compared to everything face so far Harrington was nothing more then a tiny dog that just didn't know when to stop barking. "Look Harrington, I don't know what your problem is, but Alexis can talk to whoever she wants. And for that matter so can I." he stated calmly, but firmly "Now if you wouldn't mind, we were in the middle of a conversation. Now, you were saying Lex?"

Harrington somehow got right between them in the blink of an eye "Lex?! What is that?! Some kind of pet name?! Where did it come from?! What's it mean?! What's it short for?!" he asked all in one breath.

"Alexis." Syrus answered like it was obvious, which it was.

"Sure! You'd like me to believe that wouldn't you?" Harrington asked sarcastically "Well I don't! I don't believe anything you say!"

[What is this guy's damage?!] Syrus thought to himself getting a little freaked out.

"Which is why..This little huddle is over!" Harrington stated with fight in his tone.

"Fine! Then just leave me alone already!" Syrus exclaimed, getting fed up with this lunatic.

"No way punk! Know your sports! When a huddle is over you make your play! You don't run away!" Harrington stated like it was gossple. "It's time to settle this like men!"

"Let me guess, with a Tennis match right?" Syrus asked with snark.

"No way! With a duel! And the winner becomes Alexis's fiance!"

Syrus was both floored and enraged by this nutjobs audasity! Thinking that he could just decide for Alexis who she had to be with without giving her any say in the matter! Plus the fact that he thought that he could just swoop in a steal his woman?! Not on his watch! He looked towards Alexis with a familiar fire in his eye, silently asking permission to teach this jackass a lesson!

He got it with a firm nod.

"Alright Harrington! Show me how you duel!" he shouted as sparks clashed in their eyes.

choices: Syrus vs Harrington, Part 2.
1213222111111121111331121111111112111123.content:
"Hey guys, I got to know. What's fiance mean?" Jaden asked as Syrus and Harrington got ready for their duel.

Everyone stared at him like he grew a second head.

"Jaden, answer me honestly." Bastion said in shock "Did you suffer a massive head injury when you were young?"

"I don't think so." the confused duelist answered honestly "And you guys still haven't answered my question."

"A fiance is someone your engaged with." Alexis explained calmly "In other words promised to be married to someday."

"Oooohhh." he said with realization "Boy do I feel stupid."

[You are stupid!] Jazz thought in annoyance.

"By the way Lex, how do you feel about the fact that two guys are ducking it out for your hand in marriage?" Mindy asked curiously.

"Please Mindy, I'm not getting married." Alexis stated strongly, but she mouthed the word 'yet' to her privately while giving a subtle gesture towards Syrus to let her know that she did plan to marry him someday, no matter how this duel played out. "The only reason I'm still here is because I want to see Syrus teach that arrogant ass a lesson."

"I'm afraid that might be easier said then done." Bastion said with a calculating look in his eye.

"What do you mean?" Jazz asked.

"Because Harrington is one of the best duelist's on campus." Bastion explained "In fact, his skills are on par with Zane."

"So?" Jaden shrugged like it was no big deal "Syrus beat Zane. So he should be able to beat this guy no problem."

"You forget that Syrus grew up with Zane, Jaden." Bastion pointed out "He's witnessed his brother's skills many times and was able to prepare his Deck in a way to perfectly counter everything Zane could throw at him. Where as with Harrington, he's going in blind. Plus Harrington is a rare and dangerous type of duelist known as a Spell/Trap Specialist."

"Spell/Trap Specialist?" Jaden asked curiously.

"Unlike most duelists, who use monsters as their primary means of dealing damage to their opponent, Spell/Trap Specialists counter this method by turning their opponents own play styles against them with mainly Spells and Traps. In fact, when used correctly, this sort of style can allow a duelist to wipe out their opponent's Life Points without having to summon a single monster." Bastion continued.

"Whoa!! For real?!" Jaden asked in astonishment.

"Yes. And since it's a play style that's perfectly made to counter conventional means of dueling, there aren't many ways for most duelists to effectively fight back against it, let alone win. In fact, only 2 other duelists have ever managed to defeat Harrington, Zane and Atticus Rhodes, Alexis's older brother."

Hearing the name of her brother sent a painful jolt through Alexis's heart, as it always did when he was brought up. But thanks to Syrus coming into her life the pain became easier to manage. It didn't hurt any less mind you, but at least now she had someone she trusted completely to confined in whenever the grief became to much to bare.

"So what are you saying? That Syrus can't win?" Jaden asked starting to get worried for his best friend.

"I'm saying that it's going to be an uphill battle to be sure, but not impossible." Bastion clarified "After all, Syrus is a clever duelist himself. If there is anyone who can think outside the box enough to find a way to defeat Harrington, it's him."

(Meanwhile on the court)

Syrus LP: 4000
Harrington LP: 4000

"So, are you ready to be put in your place you little punk?!" Harrington asked aggressively.

"More like ready to kick your ass!" Syrus countered "You may outclass me when it comes to sports, but when it comes to dueling, I'm the school's MVP!"

"We'll see about that! Let's do this!" Harrington sounded off as he made the first draw "First, I'm going to volley a Spell Card at you! Service Ace!"

[Even his cards are based on Tennis?] Syrus thought to himself with a sweat drop.

"Here's how it works, I pick a random card from my hand and you have to guess whether it's a Spell, Trap or Monster! If you guess right your fine! But if you guess wrong, then get served a stinging 1500 points of damage!" Harrington explained with a cocky grin.

[Aw man! I suck at guessing games!] Syrus cried out in his head, but remained steadfast [Okay Syrus, just remember what Jaden taught you! Go with you gut and stick to it!] "I choose Spell Card!"

This seemed to catch Harrington off guard before is cocky grin returned "Are you sure? You can still change your mind?" he offered.

This threw Syrus for a loop for second, but he regained his composure "No! I'm sticking to my answer!"

"Hehehe!! Sorry shorty, but it's a Monster!" Harrington revealed smugly "Now then, let's remove this card from play, and let loose Service Ace!"

A ball of light was launched towards Syrus that kicked up a powerful explosion when it hit it's target.

Syrus LP: 4000-2500

"And now I'll place this card face-down to finish the set! Your serve!" Harrington called out.

Syrus silently glared at his opponent as he made his first draw.

Syrus's Hand: Patroid, Cyber Dragon, Cyber Dragon, Cyber Twin Dragon, Polymerization, Emergency Provisions.

[Alright! With this hand I can end this duel no sweat!] Syrus thought excitedly to himself, before he remembered the face-down Harrington had. While his cards had a weird gimmick, they were quite powerful. Whose to say that card, that was undoubtedly a Trap, could be even worse then his first Spell? [Well, only one way to find out.] "I summon Patriod, in Attack Mode!"

Patroid ATK: 1200

"And thanks to his special ability, I can take a peak at one of my opponent's face-down cards once per turn! So let's see what kind of trap you're hiding Harrington!" he exclaimed with a cocky smirk of his own.

Harrington gritted his teeth angrily as his trap was revealed to his opponent, Receive Ace. A Trap that can only be activated when an opponent declares a direct attack. It not only negates the attack, but inflects 1500 points of damage to the opponent. The drawback is that the player has to discard the top 3 cards in their deck to the graveyard.

As Syrus looked over the traps effects, he was starting to understand how his opponent dueled. And figured out that his normal style of play wasn't going to work against him. So he would have to try a different strategy. It was risky, but it was the only way he knew he could win.

"I end my turn!" the short champion declared.

He needed to wait. And only attack when he had the right cards in hand.

You not our school's MVP! You're not even qualified to be this school's water boy!" Harrington ranted with real vitriol in his tone.

"I was originally here to make sure Syrus was serving his punishment. But now it seems that Harrington will be giving him a more fitting punishment by defeating him and ridding him of his undeserved title as School Champion." Crowler explained with his trademark snobbish tone.

"And I'm here to help treat any injuries that may occur." Fonda chimed in hoping to lighten up the uncomfortable atmosphere "Sports can get intense after all."

"Hey! Don't go counting Syrus out yet!" Jaden exclaimed in defense for his best friend.

"Yeah! This duel isn't over yet!" Jazz added in support of her secret lover.

"Syrus is just getting warmed up!" Mindy pitched in her 2 cents for her short boyfriend.

All the while Alexis watched on in silence. She didn't need to say anything to show her support for Syrus. To her, it was an unspoken truth that he could overcome anything.

(Back to the duel)

Syrus glared at the smug athlete across the field, who looked so sure that he had this duel in the bag. But it wasn't over yet as he made his draw. Cycroid, it may not be of use yet, but it could come in handy next turn "I summon Cycriod, in Attack Mode!"

Cycroid ATK: 800

"And I end my turn!"

"Hahahaha!! That's the best you can come up with?!" Harrington said mockingly as he made his draw. And once he saw one of his favorite cards, he knew he had this duel in the bag. "Let me show you how a real duelist makes a move! I summon The Big Sever in Attack Mode!"

Big Sever ATK: 300

"And the reason his serve is so big is because he can attack you directly! Show him Sever with Spiked Serve!" he commanded his monster.

The cyborg athlete did as his coach instructed, serving an explosive spiked ball right at Syrus with no mercy.

Syrus LP: 2000-1700

"Oh, and by the way! Big Server's special ability allows me to draw a Service Ace from my deck, and place it in my hand after he makes his attack! You can draw a card too, but it won't matter! Since there's no possible way it will be a card as good as Service Ace!"

Syrus kept his cool despite all the mockery being thrown his way as he made his draw. Mystical Space Typhoon! Just the card he needed to shut this jackass up!

"But I'm not done yet, cause now I play the Service Ace I just drew! So, want to see if third times the charm?" he asked while smugly waving the card he picked.

"It's a Trap!" Syrus called out.

"Ooooh!" Harrington gave a mocking cringe "Strike 3! It's a Spell Card!"

"Well she loves me!" Harrington stated to rub salt in the wounds "But then again, most girls do!"

"Yeah. And every single one of them were no doubt paid to love you." Syrus with a savage smirk.

(Meanwhile with the others)

Everyone's eye widened at hearing that brutal burn.

"Ouch!" Jaden summed up everyone's thoughts perfectly.

Syrus LP: 1700-200

"And just so you understand just how much out of your league you are, I equip Big Server with the Spell Card, Giant Racket! This spell has a handy one time power that allows any monster it's equipped to, to reduce all battle damage to zero! Do you get it now you little punk?! You never had a chance against me!"

"Never heard that one before." Syrus said with calm sarcasm.

Harrington was getting tired of this short-stack still not understanding his place, so he decided that the only way he'll learn is by him spelling it out for him.

"Listen you worthless piece of Slifer shit! Because it's time that you understand how the world works! There's only 2 kinds of people, Winners and Losers! Those who are meant for greatness, and those who are meant to be nothing more then stepping stones for the winner's path to destiny! And as a Rosewood, I'm a natural born winner! Where as you are just a pathetic nobody! You're a loser! You're always going to be a loser and guys like me are going to step on you for the rest of your worthless life! As is our right and privilege! And once this duel is over, I will claim Alexis as my rightful prize. And as for you, well if you get on your hands and knees and beg for mercy, I might just hire you as my towel boy! If I were you I'd take the offer, cause it's the best job you're ever going to get!"

Syrus was silent as he made his final draw.

"I mean, for most of my life it seemed to be a fact that I would never be as good as Zane. Everything always came easy to him, and it's not like he didn't work hard to get even better, cause he did. Everyone who saw him could tell that he had a bright future ahead of him. Where as with me, most people take one look at me and all they can see is Zane's younger brother. And that was if they bothered to notice me at all. You could say that Zane was born lucky, while I was lucky to be born."

Hearing the calm tone in Syrus's speech gave Harrington an uncomfortable feeling. It didn't sound like the tone of a loser realizing how pathetic he was, like he wanted it to be. But more like a young man just looking back on his younger years with nostalgia. It was like he was not in the middle of a hopeless situation, but just another regular day in his life. "What's your point?" he couldn't help but ask.

"My point, is that I've learned something important since defeating Zane. Nobody is ever 'meant for greatness' like you said. Greatness is something you earn by overcoming any obstacle that stands between you and your goals. It's earned by never giving up even when it seems impossible to move forward. Or pointless to even keep trying. I mean just look at Yugi Moto, The King Of Games himself. He didn't start off as the best duelist in the world, he started off as a high school kid who helped his grandpa run a humble game shop. He had to overcome all kinds of obstacles and challenges that even he nearly lost to. He even had his fair share of losses over his career. But the one thing that made him the greatest in the world, is that no matter what the odds, even when things seemed to be at their darkest, he found the will within himself to carry on and find a way to win the day."

"Nice story." Harrington stated with a bored tone "But I don't see how that has anything to do with our duel here."

"Actually, it has a lot to do with our duel." Syrus said as he remembered something important. "Because Yugi's first true duel was against Seto Kiba, one of the richest people on earth and the best duelist at the time. Do you see where I'm going with this?"

Harrington thought for a second before it came to him "Hold on! Are you saying that I'm Seto and you're Yugi in this scenario?!" he asked in shock as he knew about how that duel ended.

"Yep." Syrus answered with a confident grin.

"What are saying?! That you somehow got Exodia in your hand?!" he asked in disbelief "Cause that's the only way I can see you possibly winning this duel!"

Syrus let out a chuckle "Of course not!"

Harrinton sighed in relief.

"But what cards I do have, will work just as good." Syrus added as his grin widened "First, I play Mystical Space Typhoon to destroy your Receive Ace!"

And just like that, Harrington's face-down and confidence shattered to pieces.

"Next, I sacrifice Cycriod to summon Cyber Dragon!"

Cyber Dragon ATK: 2100

WHAT?!?!" Harrington exclaimed in horror "BUT I THOUGHT THAT ZANE GIVING YOU THOSE CARDS WAS JUST A RUMOR!!!"

"Afraid not. Cause now I play Polymerization to fuse my Cyber Dragon on the field and the one in my hand to form, Cyber Twin Dragon!!"

Cyber Twin Dragon ATK: 2800

"NO!!! THIS CAN'T BE HAPPENING!!!" Harrington shouted as all his delusions were being torn apart before his eyes.

"Patriod! Attack with Siren Smasher!" Syrus commanded his police car monster.

And he did so with gusto, forcing The Big Server to use the Giant Racket to delay his inevitable destruction.

"Now, Cyber Twin Dragon, Finish it with Double Strident Blast!!" Syrus commanded one of his best monsters.

The robotic dragon carried out it's orders gladly, obliterating both Harrington's Life Points and his ego all in one fell swoop.

Harrington LP: 4000-0

"And that's how you duel!" Syrus said his victory catchphrase with a triumphant fist raised.

"But she was my soulmate!!" Harrington sobbed as tears of defeat flowed down his face "My first draft pick!! My future baby mama!! How could you take her from me?!" he exclaimed pathetically.

"She was never yours to begin with." Syrus explained calmly "And just so you know, trying to decide for someone else who they're going to marry, while giving them absolutely no say in the matter, is seriously messed up dude. In fact I would even go so far as to say it should be illegal."

"I wholeheartedly agree." Crowler said with an honestly chilling tone as he approached the duelists.

Syrus actually felt intimidated by that serious look of anger in his good eye.

"Harrington Rosewood. Am I to understand that you tried to make Alexis Rhodes, one of my best students, into some sort of trophy? A prize to be won?" the Docter in dueling asked with a cold fury in his voice.

Harrington was also surprised and frightened at this new side of Crowler as he scrambled to come up with an answer "W-well Doc, you see-" he was silenced by Crowler raising an open hand in a stop sign.

"Mr. Rosewood, I'm sure you know that this school has very strict rules on how duels are to be conducted. Rules that is if are ever broken are grounds for expulsion. And by trying to lay claim to Ms. Rhodes as some sort of object that can be sold off as some kind prize clearly shows that you have no respect for her, or the sanctity of this academy. And for that, I have no choice but to expel you." Crowler stated with finality.

Harrington was flabbergasted at hearing this "Say what?! You can't do this to me!! Do you have any idea what my family will do when they hear about this?!"

"Quite frankly I don't give a damn, because there is nothing they can do. You family may be rich, but their business is in sports, not dueling. So they would have no legal leg to stand on if they tried to sue or whatever it is you think they can do to threaten this school. Now, go pack your things and get out of my sight." Crowler commanded strongly.

Harrington stood there in complete shock for a moment before walking off to do as he was told, completely defeated.

Syrus was not fairing much better and was worried that he might be next. As while he didn't intend to follow through with the terms of victory, he still dueled with the terms set. Which meant if Crowler wanted to, he could expel him too.

"Syrus Trusedale!" Crowler called out.

Syrus stood at attention and prayed that Crowler would show mercy as he approached him.

"Please understand that what I'm about to say is not easy for me." Crowler said firmly, but not with the same coldness as before. "I'm sorry." he said with a slight bow.

Syrus blinked in surprise "Huh?"

"I understand that I come across as an elitist snob that does everything in his power to make things as hard as possible for students that I consider to be unworthy of attending this school. But understand that my views and methods are not out of malice. Because the truth is, I want nothing more then for all of my students to excel, to be the best they can be. But I also know that life is never going to make it easy for any of them if they truly want to succeed, so I make it my mission to trow everything I can at them so that they have the opportunity to prove themselves. And for the longest time the students of the Slifer dorm always fell short of my expectations. But that's now starting to change." Crowler explained.

Syrus was honestly impressed with how wise Crowler was sounding right now, like he was really a Doctor.

"Ever since you defeated Zane, you have inspired the other Slifers to not only improve, but take pride in their dorm. You should hear what some of them are saying whenever they manage to defeat someone of a higher rank. 'I'm proud to be a Slifer'. Never in all my years of teaching at this school have I heard such a thing. And it's all thanks to you." Crowler said with pride before turning remorseful "And here I was not 5 minutes ago thinking that having a Slifer as our Top Duelist was a disgrace to our image. But now, it's clear to me that the color of your vest doesn't truly show your potential, but your actions and skill does. And you might have the most potential of any student I've ever seen."

Syrus was starting to blush from all the praise "Gosh Dr. Crowler, I don't know what to say." he said as he rubbed the back of his head sheepishly.

"You don't need to say anything." Crowler said with a proud smile "Just do me a favor and continue to excel and do this academy proud."

Syrus gave a nod and salute "Yes sir!"

Crowler gave a nod and waled off to take care of some work regarding Harrington's expulsion. As he left the court he turned his head to see Syrus getting a swarm of congratulations from his friends "Godspeed, Syrus Truesdale." he said to himself. Happy to know that the future of the academy was looking bright.
1213222111111121111331121111111112111123.title: Playing Hard Vs Playing Smart, Finale.

choices: Let Jaden handle this like in canon.
Syrus steps up once again.
Skip the duel.
1213222111111121111331121111111112111123.content:
It's been a crazy 5 weeks since Syrus's duel with Harrington. With him and Jaden helping a Ra student named Brier, who was the only person in the school even shorter then Syrus, get over his stage fright to help him become a better duelist. As well as stopping him from stealing other student's best cards with his best friend Beauregard masquerading as The Duel Giant.

And stopping a Tarzan wannabe named Damon from stealing the only good sandwich served on Sandwich Day. That was a really weird night, even for them.

But now things were only going to get more exciting, as the most famous dueling deck in the world, Yugi Moto's, was going on tour. And it's first stop was Duel Academy. As you can imagine the whole school was in a excited buzz as it was any young duelist's dream to see The King Of Games Deck. So tickets to see the deck were quickly sold out, with the last one being held as a prize in a duel between Jaden and a Ra Yellow Copycat Duelist named Dimitri.

And the latest deck Dimitri was copying was Syrus's.

Jaden LP: 800
Dimitri LP: 2000

Jaden had Elemental Hero: Clayman in Defense Mode, as well as a face-down on his side of the field. Meanwhile Dimitri had Life Force in play and was about to make a summon.

"Now I summon Submarineroid in Attack Mode!" Dimitri said in his best Syrus voice as he copied one of the signature moves of Duel Academy's Champion.

Submarineroid ATK: 800

"And now I use his special ability to attack you directly and finish this duel!" the copycat exclaimed as he made his final command.

"Nice try, but you just allowed me to play Mirror force!" Jaden shouted with a grin as his trap turned Dimitri's sure fire win against him.

"No way!" Dimitri cried out in dismay as Submarineriod was destroyed with his own torpedo.

"Oh yes way! And now it's my turn!" Jaden exclaimed as he made his draw. And his grin only widened as he saw the card now in his hand "Sweet! Now I play Fusion Gate! With this field spell I can summon any Fusion Monster without needing Polymerization! And I choose to fuse my Clayman on the field and Burstinatrix in my hand to create, The Elemental Hero: Rampart Blaster!"

Rampart Blaster ATK: 2000

"Now, Rampart Blaster, lets really finish it with Rampart Barrage!" he said with great excitement as his hero did as commanded, letting loose a volley of missiles that laid waste to the rest of Dimitri's Life Points.

Dimitri LP: 2000-0

"That's game!" Jaden said his catchphrase along with his signature pose in victory "Nice moves by the way! You really copied Syrus's style to a T!" he complemented honestly.

"And yet, I still lost." Dimitri said in his real voice with great frustration. He was so sure that copying the best student on campus's deck would allow him to finally win, but it failed. Just like all the others. "Well I've had it!" he shouted as he ran off to his room to vent his frustrations in privacy.

Jaden blinked in confusion "Was it something I said?"

"No Jaden." Bastion answered for his confused friend "Dimitri is just frustrated with himself. While his skills are formidable, he just never seems to be able to win. No matter who he tries to copy."

"Well then, maybe he should stop copying and start making his own deck. Start forming his own style." Syrus advised.

"Yes I agree." Bastion said with a nod "I've even tried to tell him the same thing a number of times, but he never seems to listen."

"Oh boys!" Ms. Dorthy called out while playfully waving the prized ticket "Aren't you forgetting something?"

"Oh yeah! I was so sucked into the duel I completely forgot why I started it!" Jaden said with realization as he took the ticket and handed it towards Syrus "Here you go pal!"

"Thanks Jay! You're the best!" Syrus said gratefully.

"All though, I would've figured you would be one of the first people with a ticket." Jaden said thoughtfully.

"And I would've been..but.." Syrus trailed off with a bashful blush "I had a..um..previous engagement."

Jaden had a shit eating grin on his face "Let me guess..too busy making out with one of your girlfriends to make it in time?" he teased.

"..Yes." Syrus said quietly with a blush.

"Who was it? Alexis, Jazz or Mindy?" Jaden egged on.

"..All three." Syrus said with his face turning completely red.

Jaden let out an impressed whistle "Go Sy."

"It never ceases to amaze me how you were able to charm 3 lovely young women into dating you at the same time." Bastion said with sincere awe "How did you do it?"

"I have no idea." was all Syrus could say.

(Later that night)

Jaden was naturally too impatient to just wait and see the deck with everyone else tomorrow. So he convinced Syrus and Chumely to go see the deck early, only to find that it had been stolen. So Syrus called everyone he knew to let them all in on the search, to which the agreed to help without hesitation. And after almost an hour of searching they all met at the bridge.

"The dorms are clear." Bastion informed.

"And so are the classrooms." Zane added.

"Maybe the thief is somewhere in the woods." Mindy theorized.

"I sure hope not." Jazz commented as she did not want to go running into the woods at night again.

"Hold on, where's Chumely?" Syrus asked, noticing their heavy weight friend was missing.

"Aaaahhhhh!!" shouted a familiar voice near the shore.

"That's Chumely!" Jaden exclaimed "He sounds like he's in trouble!"

"Let's move!" Syrus commanded as he ran to investigate, with his friends right behind him.

They found Chumely laying on his back near a stone on the shore with a Duel Disk equipped to his arm, looking soundly defeated.

"Chumely!!" Alexis cried out in worry "Are you alright?

"He has it." Chumely grunted as he sat up right "He has the deck. I tried to win it back, but he's too good."

"Not just good young duelist, the best." said a voice that honestly was almost a dead ringer for Yugi's voice.

The other duelists looked up to see, that it was Dimitri, with his blazer open to reveal a black shirt with The Millennium Puzzle printed on it. A tattered scarf acting as a cape blowing in the wind. And he had a look of absolute confidence on his face. Like there was nothing he couldn't do, no one who could challenge him now. And with good reason.

He now wielded the most famous deck in the world.

"So, who wants to challenge the king next?" Dimitri asked in his new voice.
1213222111111121111331121111111112111123.title: A Legendary Deck Comes To Duel Academy!

choices: Syrus vs Dimitri, Part 2.
1213222111111121111331121111111112111123.content:
Both Jaden and Syrus gave a hard stare at the copycat who stole the cards of every duelist's idol, both wanting to teach him a lesson and test their skills against arguably the most powerful deck in the world. They shared a look and a nod, both knowing how to decide who will step up to the challenge.

"Rock, Paper, Scissors!" they both shouted at the same time, only for them both to pick scissors. "Rock, Paper, Scissors!" Another draw, this time rock.

Everyone either face faulted or sweat dropped at the best friend's silly game in this serious situation. Their game continued for about a minute before Syrus finally won with paper covering rock.

"Yes!!" the short champion cheered as he jumped onto the stone, with Duel Disk and deck ready to battle.

"Aw man!" Jaden exclaimed glumly as a comical rain cloud form over his head "I should've called for best 2 out of 3." he pouted.

"You do realize that Syrus is going up against the best deck in the world, right?!" Bastion asked incredulously.

"Of course I do! Lucky bastard." Jaden's pout deepened.

"Plus there is the fact that Dimitri copied Syrus earlier, which means he knows all his tricks and strategies." Zane pointed out grimly.

"But Syrus can still win, right?" Mindy asked tentatively.

"Yeah, he's pulled off near impossible wins before." Jazz said with the same uncertainty.

"But so has Yugi." Bastion reminded "That is the main reason his deck is so legendary. With his cards, he was able to pull off victories that by all rights should've been impossible. It's the legacy of what his career is based on, 'believe in the heart of the cards and all is possible'. And Dimitri has no doubt obsessively studied all of Yugi's own strategies, which means at this point, their is more then likely no other duelist besides Yugi himself that can defeat Dimitri now."

With all these odds staked against him, everyone was worried that Syurs wouldn't stand a chance. Even Alexis's normally unshakable belief in her knight's skills was shaken ever so slightly. But she remained held fast to her faith that Syrus would find a way to overcome what was undoubtedly going to be his greatest test yet.

(Now to the duel)

"Before we get started, Dimitri I want you to promise that if I win, not only will you return Yugi's Deck, but you won't ever try to copy other peoples decks again." Syrus bargained.

The copycat scoffed "Dimitri? I'm afraid you're mistaken, I'm Yugi!" he said in his Yugi voice.

"Say what?" Syrus asked getting weirded out by his clearly untrue claim.

"Dimitri is some child who would study other peoples decks and copy them! But with these cards, I'm not copying The King Of Games. I truly am The King Of Games!" 'Yugi' declared as the waves made a big splash behind him for dramatic effect.

Hearing this lit a fire of righteous fury in Syrus's heart. As it was clear to him now that, no logic or reason would work on Dimitri now. And he honestly didn't deserve mercy anymore as far as Syrus was concerned. Stealing Yugi's Deck is one thing, but trying to steal his identity on top of that? That was crossing the line.

And now Syrus was going to do everything in his power to end this man's whole career.

"Well, in that case 'Yugi'," Syrus said with a voice cold and hard as steel as he activated his Duel Disk "show me how you duel."

"I believe the more appropriate phrase is, It's Time To Duel!" Dimitri exclaimed Yugi's iconic catchphrase like he owned it.

[That's it. I'm going to destroy him.] Syrus thought darkly to himself as he got his hand ready.

Syrus LP: 4000
Dimitri LP: 4000

Syrus made the first draw.

Syrus's Hand: Jetriod, Gyriod, Mirror Force, Steamriod, Rush Recklessly, Draining Shield.

A plan already started to form as Syrus choose his first move "I place a monster face-down in Defense Mode and end my turn." he said with calm tone, much like his brother.

"Very well then!" Dimitri stated as he made his first draw "First I play Pot Of Greed to draw 2 more cards! Then I summon Gazelle, The King Of Mythical Beasts!"

Gazelle, King Of Mythical Beasts ATK: 1500

"Next I use Polymerization to fuse Gazelle from my field and Berfoment from my hand to form, Chimera, The Flying Mythical Beast!"

Chimera, The Flying Mythical Beast ATK: 2100

"But that's not all! Now I play the Spell Card, Swords Of Revealing Light!"

One of Yugi's most infamous spells, which makes it so his opponent's monsters can't attack for 3 turns. But his monsters still can.

"Now Chimera, attack with Pulverizing Pounce!"

"You just activated my face-down monster's special ability, and it's my old friend Jetriod's, which allows me to play a Trap from my hand whenever he's attacked in battle once per turn." Syrus explained with a slight smirk as he played his trap "And I play Mirror Force!"

A powerful force field intercepted Chimera's attack from hitting his prey and released all his strength back at him, reducing him to dust.

"Impressive ability," Dimitri commended "now let me show you mine! Whenever Chimera is destroyed, I'm allow to summon back either Gazelle or Berfoment from my graveyard! And I choose Berforment!"

Berforment DEF: 1800

"Your Jetriod's effect may have saved him, but you only have so many trap cards in your hand. And with Swords of Revealing Light in effect, you still can't attack." Dimitri reminded with a confident grin.

Syrus was not intimidated as he made his draw, Submarineroid, that can definitely come in handy later. But for now it was best to build up his defense. "I place another monster face-down in Defense Mode and end my turn."

Dimitri made his draw, his grin grew slightly as it seemed that victory was already in his grasp. "Alright, first I put 1 card face -down. Then I play Monster Reincarnation!" he declared as he discarded his card to the grave to add Gazelle back to his hand. "Then I summon back to the field Gazelle in Attack Mode!"

The mythical lion was now back on the field ready to battle.

"Now Gazelle, attack his face-down monster with Roar Of Mythical Kings!" Dimitri commanded.

The beast king let out a roar that sound more like an entire Lion Pride then just one, and it kick up a mighty blast of wind that revealed Syrus's face-down monster to be Gyriod. Still standing due to his special ability.

"Just as I suspected. You instincts prove to be sharp with how you are able to build such a powerful defense. But it's not goning to save you from what I have in store for you." Dimitri promised.

Syrus was silent as he made his draw, Cyber Dragon, can't special summon it due to him have monsters already on his field. But he can summon another. And after this next turn he will be able to attack "First I place 1 card face down. Then I summon another monster face-down in Defense Mode."

Dimitri smirked as his plan could now be put into action "That summoning you just made allows me to play my Trap Card, Dark Renewal! And now by tributing one of your monsters as well as one of my own, I'm allowed to summon a Spellcaster Monster from my graveyard!"

"But, you don't have any Spellcasters in your graveyard!" Syrus called out, before he remember something "Hold on..Monster Reincarnation!"

"You catch on fast. Now, Berfoment and Jetriod, make some room for the one, the only! My friend, The Dark Magician!!" Dimitri bellowed as the monsters were sacrificed to bring for the legendary Spellcaster to the field.

Dark Magician ATK: 2500

It was at this point that Syrus was starting to get nervous. After all, he was now face to face with Yugi Moto's favorite monster. The monster that helped him prevail in countless duels that any other duelist would fail. But that didn't mean he was backing down. He come too far just to run away now.

One way or another, he was dueling to the end.
1213222111111121111331121111111112111123.title: A True Knight Vs A False King, Part 1.

choices: Syrus vs Dimitri, Part 3.
1213222111111121111331121111111112111123.content:
Syrus LP: 4000
Dimitri LP: 4000

Everyone present watching this duel felt a chill in the air, as they all bore witness to one of the most iconic monsters in the world about to do battle. And if there's one thing that any duelist knows, even if they've been living under a rock for the last 10 years, is that when The Dark Magician is brought to the field, shit is about to get real.

And Syrus couldn't help but gulp in nervousness as the legendary Spellcaster seemed to stare right at him with blue eyes full of power and wisdom. Power and wisdom that he was going to use to destroy the young knight.

But still, Syrus had a secret weapon that he had face-down, and he couldn't wait to use it for the first time. "I end my turn!" Syrus shouted, ready for the duel to truly begin.

"Very well!" Dimitri said, still in his Yugi persona as he made his draw. And his smirk widened ever so slightly as he saw the card he just drew will shatter Syrus's defenses. "First, I play the Spell Card, Thousand Knifes! With this, as long as I have Dark Magician on the field, I can destroy any 1 monster you control! And I choose to destroy Gyriod!"

With a wave of his staff, Dark Magician let loose a literal rain of blades that reduced Gyriod to nothing.

"And now, Dark Magician attack! Dark Magic Attack!" Dimitri commanded.

The sorcerer did as commanded, unleashing a ball of pure dark energy that would annihilate Syrus's face-down monster. If it wasn't for his trap that is.

"I activate Draining Shield!" Syrus exclaimed as his newest trap created a green force field that not only stopped The Dark Magician's attack, but added the value of his ATK to his Life Points.

Syrus LP: 4000-6500

"What?!" Dimitri exclaimed in disbelief, almost breaking character "You shouldn't have that card in your deck!" And he should know since he copied it earlier today.

" I do now. It's a new one I got as part of a Booster Pack for Christmas." Syrus admitted with a confident smirk "And trust me, that pack has turned my deck into a whole new beast." he boasted.

Dimitri gritted his teeth in frustration, but then remembered the deck he wielded and regained his composure."Impressive, but my deck is prepared for anything. So go ahead and make your move young duelist, because soon you will see the true power of The Heart Of The Cards!" he exclaimed like he actually believed in what he was saying.

Syrus clenched a fist in anger at Dimitri's mockery of Yugi's true beliefs. It didn't matter if the copycat understood how much he was disgracing the name of the greatest duelist in the world, because Syrus was going to make sure he understood by crushing his delusions to dust. He made his draw, Emergency Provisions, could come in handy in pinch.

"First, I flip my Submarineriod to Attack Mode!" he called out.

Submarineriod ATK: 800

"Then I summon Steamriod in Attack Mode!"

Steamriod ATK: 1800

"Now I going to give Steamriod a boost with Rush Recklessly! This will increase his ATK by 700 for 1 turn, making him a match for The Dark Magician! And thanks to his special ability he also gains 500 ATK when he makes his attack! Now go Steamriod! Attack Dark Magician!"

Steamriod ATK: 1800-3000

Syrus's little engine that could, charged forward at full speed and turned the famous sorcerer into roadkill.

Dimtri LP: 4000-3500

"Now Submarineriod, attack Dimitri directly!!" Syrus commanded with great fervor.

The sentient sub did as instructed, and let loose a torpedo straight at the copycat thief. And after he made his attack, he switch to Defense Mode as his effect allowed.

Submarineriod DEF: 1800

Dimitri LP: 3500-2700

"You move, 'Yugi'." he said with mockingly.

(Meanwhile with the others)

Everyone was in complete shock and awe at what they were seeing. Not only was Syrus holding his own against Yugi's Deck, he was winning. No, dominating is a more accurate word. It was a something that none of them, not even Alexis, could have ever imagined was possible. But there it was, clear as day.

Jaden: Oh.

Zane: My.

Chumely: Fucking

Bastion: God.

That pretty much summed up the thoughts of everyone watching, which had grown from 7 to nearly 100 as more students and faculty had heard about Yugi's Deck being stolen and went to look for it. What they found instead was one of the most unbelievable sights anyone one of them had ever seen.

Yugi's Deck was losing.

Alexis, Jazz and Mindy were not only in awe, but unbelievably turned on at seeing their lover dominating this duel.

[He is SO getting laid after this!] they all thought at the same time with great arousal.

(Back to the duel)

Dimitri was starting to sweat nervously as it seemed that not even Yugi's Deck was going to be enough for him to let him win. But as he made his draw, he regained his nerv "I play Monster Reborn to bring back Dark Magician!" he called out in triumph.

And sure enough, Yugi's favorite monster was back on the field ready for round 2.

"Now Dark Magician, destroy Submarineriod!"

The powerful wizard did as commanded, as with but a wave of his hand he cause the sub to explode.

"Now Gazelle, Attack Steamriod!"

The mythical lion moved with lightning speed and slashed the living engine with claws sharper then razors. And since Steamriod's effect reduces his ATK by 500 whenever a monster attacks him, it was enough to defeat him.

And leave Syrus wide open.

Syrus LP: 6500-6300

"You've done well against me so far young duelist, but now it's time for you to see the true power of my deck!!" Dimtri bellowed with new found confidence, before also let out a short laugh of victory.

Syrus glared at the false king with a hotter fire in his eye then before. As it was now clear to him, and everyone watching, that this duel was far from over.
1213222111111121111331121111111112111123.title: A True Knight Vs A False King, Part 2.

choices: Syrus vs Dimitri, Part 4.
1213222111111121111331121111111112111123.content:
Syrus LP: 6300
Dimitri LP: 2700

Syrus made his draw, Training Wheels, if nothing else he had a card to discard for Emergency Provisions if he needed it. "You may have defeated all my monsters Dimitri, but that just allows me to special summon Cyber Dragon in Attack Mode!" he exclaimed as he brought one of his own deck's heavy hitters onto the field.

Cyber Dragon ATK: 2100

"It may not be enough to take on Dark Magician, but I can still take down Gazelle! Go Cyber Dragon, Strident Blast!"

The robotic dragon did as instructed, letting loose a powerful blast of fire that reduced The King Of Mythical Beasts to ashes.

Dimitri LP: 2700-2100

"Now, do your worst!" he demanded as an aura of fire seemed to engulf the short champion "I can take it!"

Dimitri smirked as he made his draw, which only grew as he saw the card that will not only turn this duel completely in his favor, but prove that he was worthy to be called The King Of Games. "You want my worst, well you're about to get it! I play Dedication Through Light And Darkness!" he declared "This allows me to take my Dark Magician, and turn him into something even more powerful! The Dark Magician Of Chaos!"

An aura of raw magic swarmed The Dark Magician, and then in a flash of dark light the magic transformed him into one of his most powerful incarnations.

Dark Magician Of Chaos ATK: 2800

"And just so you know, he's not only stronger then the original Dark Magician, but he has a few special abilities that make him one of the most powerful creatures you'll ever see." Dimitri said with a grin "Such as when he destroys a monster in battle, that card isn't sent to the graveyard. It's banished from the game entirely!"

Hearing this caused Syrus eyes to widen in shock, as it was now really starting to become clear to him why Yugi's Deck was so legendary. After all, hearing about and seeing it in action are two very different things.

"Now, Chaos Magician attack! Scepter Strike!"

The Chaos sorcerer did as commanded, launching a ball of blue magic energy that not only destroyed Syrus's dragon, but sent it's pieces to a different dimension.

Syrus LP: 6300-5600

"And now for Chaos Magician's other special ability, which allows me to draw any Spell Card from my graveyard and add it to my hand at the end of my turn. And I choose, Monster Reborn!" he revealed said card to show it was real.

"Now hold on! That's cheating! The rules say you can't use Monster Reborn more then once!" Syrus called out.

Dimitri just gave a chuckle "Actually, the rule states that a duelist is allowed to only have 1 Monster Reborn in their deck." he pointed out "I just simply found a way to use that same Monster Reborn again."

"Hax! I call hax!" Syrus shouted with indignation. As not only did he permanently lose one of his best monster, at least till the end of the duel, but Dimitri just found a way to legally use one of the most powerful Spell Cards in all of Duel Monsters as many times as he wants.

At this point it didn't even matter if Syrus found a way to defeat Chaos Magician, because Dimitri could just bring him back with Monster Reborn and then he could just use that special ability to bring back Monster Reborn and the cycle would just start over again. Plus, with one of his Cyber Dragons banished, he didn't have anyway to summon Cyber End Dragon, one of the only monster he had who could defeat Chaos Magician.

"Do you give up?" Dimitri asked curiously.

If Syrus were to be honest, he was tempted to give up. He thought he was ready to take on Yugi's Deck, but now after seeing what it was truly capable of, he was now starting to think he never had a chance. But then he looked behind him, to see his friends, his brother, his lovers, his queen, looking on with faith in him that can still find a way to win. He then looked back at Dimitri, and he swore he could see the silhouette of Yugi Moto himself behind his copycat, looking on with judging eyes.

But most of all, he remembered his oath.

"NEVER!!!" Syrus bellowed as his fire returned.

"That's the spirit!" Dimitri said with genuine joy as this duel was just getting good. "Never give up! Believe in The Heart Of The Cards!"

"SHOVE IT!!!" Syrus shouted with fury as he made his draw, and he saw another one of the cards from his Booster Pack that Alexis, Jazz and Mindy all bought him for Christmas.

Vehicroid Connection Zone.

He then remembered that he now had 2 other ace monsters in his deck. And one of them, under the right circumstances could put even all his Cyber Dragons to shame. He just needed to hold out and play his cards right so that he can allow it to make it's grand debut.

"I play Emergency Provisions!" he exclaimed as he discarded Training Wheels, the only other card left in his hand.

Syrus LP: 5600-6600

"And I end my turn!"

"Very well then." Dimitri said as he made his draw "I now play the Card Of Sanctity! It forces us both to banish all other cards in our hands till we have 1 left, then draw until we both have 6 cards in our hand."

Since Syrus only hand 1 card he didn't need to worry about losing one the key pieces for his secret weapon, and Dimitri no doubt kept Monster Reborn as they both drew their new hands.

"Excellent! Just drew Watapon!" the copycat said happily "You see, whenever Watapon is brought to my hand due a card effect I'm allow to automatically summon him! But he won't be out for long, because I'm going to sacrifice him to summon for Dark Magician Girl!"

And with that, the first crush of every young male duelist ever (and more the a few girls) was brought to the field with a radiant smile on her lovely face.

Dark Magician Girl ATK: 2000

"Woah!" Syrus said with awe as nearly all his rage was gone as he gazed upon the woman who kick started his puberty. "She's even cuter in person."

"She has beauty and brawn. After all, her special ability allows her to gain 300 ATK points for every Dark Magician I have in the graveyard." Dimitri reminded.

Dark Magician Girl ATK: 2000-2300

"But that's not all, because now I play the Spell Card, Spider Web! This allows me to take 1 Spell Card you used last turn and place it in my hand! And I choose Emergency Provisions!"

A string of spider silk shot into Syrus's graveyard and plucked the Spell Card of the false king's choosing, bringing to his waiting hand.

"Now I'm going to play Emergency Provisions by discarding 3 Spell Cards from my hand!"

Dimitri LP: 2100-5100

"And now, Chaos Magician! Dark Magician Girl! Combine attack! Dark Burning Scepter Strike!" Dimitri commanded.

The two masters of the dark arts did as instructed, putting their staffs together to combine their powers and unleash an almighty blast of purple energy that hit a defenseless Syrus without mercy.

Syrus LP: 6600-1500

Syrus was sure glad he already had a ton of Life Points, otherwise that attack would've wiped him out for sure.

"And with Chaos Magician's ability, I'll take back from my graveyard one of the Spell Cards I discarded last turn. And I once again choose Monster Reborn! Now then Champion Of Duel Academy, show me what you can really do!" the false king encouraged.

As Syrus made his draw and smirked saw his new hand, he knew that if he did this right, then he will become one of the few duelists who ever managed to defeat Yugi's Deck.

It was time to put this poser in his place.

1213222111111121111331121111111112111123.content:
The tension in the air was thick as nearly all the students and faculty members of Duel Academy looked on at what was one of the best duels any of them had ever seen. Not just because they were seeing The King Of Games Deck in action, but also a duelist who was able to match it blow for blow.

"My word." Shepard whispered in awe "This is one of the most incredible duels I've ever seen."

"Indeed." Prof. Banner stated with a similar tone as he stroke his cat. "Syrus has truly come a long way to be able to do so well against Yugi's Deck."

"And Dimitri is playing the deck very much like how Yugi himself would!" Ms. Fontaine commented with glee, as she was an avid fan of Yugi so she knew what she was talking about on how The King Of Games played his cards.

"But are we really going to just let this play out?" Dr. Crowler asked with a mixture of frustration and excitement. For while he was enjoying the duel as much as anyone, he also really wanted to punish the scum who thought they could get away with not only stealing the greatest deck in the world, but claim to be the decks rightful owner. "We have the scoundrel right where we want him! We should seize him before he tries to make a run for it!"

"Relax Crowler, Dimitri will be punished most severely when this duel is over." Shepard promised as he kept his eye on Syrus who was looking over his new hand. "And I believe Syrus will punish him during the duel as well.

"You truly think Syrus can win?!" Crowler exclaimed with disbelief, with most of his peers looking at their boss with similar expressions. "I mean, I know the boy is good, but this is Yugi's Deck we are talking about here!"

"Yugi's Deck is powerful, but not invincible." Shepard reminded them "And many things have changed in Duel Monsters since Yugi's heyday. Besides, I get the feeling that Syrus has got something very special in store."

(Back to the duel)

Syrus LP: 1500
Dimitri LP: 5100

Syrus looked at his new hand with a smirk as while Dimitri no doubt had turned things in his favor last turn, he also may have set the stage for his downfall.

Syrus's Hand: No Entry, Shield Crush, Negate Attack, Stealthriod, Truckriod, Vehicriod Connection Zone, Pot Of Greed.

"I play Pot Of Greed!" he stated as he drew 2 more cards. Expressriod and Graceful Charity.

[Excellent.] he thought to himself happily "Now I play Graceful Charity!"

He discarded Stealthriod and Truckriod since he could use Expressriod's ability to get them back when he needed them. Then he drew his 3 new cards, Drillriod, Super Vehicriod - Stealth Union, and Cyber Dragon.

[Perfect!] he shouted in his head as his smirk threatened to split his face in half with how big it was getting. But he took a deep breath to calm down as he didn't want to spoil the surprise.

"And now, I place 1 card face-down and end my turn."

Dimitri was puzzled by his opponent's odd play, but wasn't worried as he made his draw "I place 1 card face-down! And then I play Monster Reborn to bring back The Dark Magician!" he shouted.

His Spell did the trick, bringing for Yugi's favorite monster back to the field.

"Of course by doing so Dark Magician Girl loses her extra ATK, but having her master back on the field more then makes up for it."

Dark Magician Girl ATK: 2300-2000

"Now Dark Magician Girl attack!" he commanded.

"Nice try, but no dice." Syrus smirked as his master plan was coming together "I activate No Entry!"

"Another new card?!" Dimitri asked in shock.

"That's right! It forces all of your monsters to switch to Defense Mode and forces you to discard 1 card from your hand! "Syrus informed.

The spellcasters grimaced as they were all forced to their knees in defensive positions.

Dark Magician DEF: 2100

Dark Magician Girl DEF: 1700

Dark Magician Of Chaos DEF: 2600

Dimitri grunted in frustration "You got off easy." he said as he discarded Kuriboh form his hand "But I will get you next time. And I still get back Monster Reborn thanks to Chaos Magician."

"Not like it's going to help you in the end." Syrus claimed as he made his draw. Mystical Space Typhoon, one of his favorites. And perfect for taking out any surprise Dimitri might have for him face-down. "Now I play Mystical Space Typoon to destroy your face-down card!"

Dimitri's card shattered like glass as the magic winds blew across the field.

"Next I activate Shield Crush! With this I can destroy any 1 monster you have in Defense Mode! And I really hate to do this, but I choose to destroy Dark Magician Girl!"

Right when Syrus made the call, everyone's favorite magical girl was vaporized by a green lazer beam.

"But I'm not done! Cause now I summon Drillriond in Attack Mode!"

Drillriod ATK: 1600

"And with his special ability, I can destroy any 1 monster in Defense Mode! Go Drillriod, attack Chaos Magician with Triple Drill Charge!"

The living drilling machine did as instructed, and use it's drill hands to savagely lift the chaos wizard by his stomach, and then use his big drill nose to dig into the helpless spellcaster's face until he exploded. Let's just be glad that all this was done by holograms, otherwise that would have been a truly gruesome sight to witness.

"And I end my turn with 1 card face-down."

Dimitri was starting to sweat nervously again, for despite having a massive advantage in Life Points and powerful monsters, Syrus was proving why he was named Duel Academy's Champion by not only standing his ground, but fighting back against the odds. But as he made his draw, the false king lost all sense of fear as it seem The Heart Of The Cards was truly on his side.

"You have proven yourself a worthy opponent Syrus Trusedale." he commended sincerely "But now, it's time to end this! For I now have the means to summon a monster so powerful that I only ever take him out for special occasions!"

Syrus was really getting tired of the fraud still continuing to believe he's actually Yugi. But he had to admit. he was curious about this so called special monster.

"I banish from my graveyard 1 Light Monster and 1 Dark Monster in order to summon from my hand, Black Luster Soldier - Envoy Of The Beginning!!" he bellowed with great fervor.

A wormhole appeared on the field that sucked in Watapon and Kuriboh, and from the wormhole came out a truly fearsome warrior that embodied everything you would imagine a knight form a legend would look like.

And Syrus would admit that as the knight looked him right in the eye, he felt a chill of dread crawl up his spine.

Black Luster Soldier - Envoy Of The Beginning ATK: 3000

"But I'm not finished yet as I now switch my Dark Magician to Attack Mode!"

The spellcaster rose from his feet and readied his staff for battle.

"And just to make sure you understand why challenging The King Of Games will always end in defeat, I use Monster Reborn to bring back form the grave Dark Magician Of Chaos!!" Dimitri bellowed as the power of Yugi's Deck was beginning to overwhelm his senses.

And thus the fabled wizard was brought back to the field to battle once more.

"And I'll have Chaos Magician take some much deserved revenge for last round! And then I'll have Black Luster Soldier finish you!! Attack!!"

The sorcerer did as commanded, launching all his power to destroy the monster who brutalized him, and scatter it's remains to the 4 winds.

But Syrus wasn't worried in the slightest "I activate Negate Attack!" he exclaimed.

Just before the magic attack could hit it's mark, it dissipated.

"WHAT?!?!" Dimitri cried out in shock as his victory was once again denied.

"Thanks to my Trap, not only is your Chaos Magician's attack wasted, but it ends you Battle Phase! So none of your other monster can attack either!" Syrus explained.

Dimitri gritted his teeth in anger "Fine!! Then I'll just finish you off next turn!! The Heart of The Cards will see to that!!"

Honestly at this point, Syrus wasn't really mad at Dimitri anymore. In fact he kind of pitied him. He just so desperately wanted to win that he was will to all this trouble. He was even truly starting to believe he really was Yugi. This guy need him help. And there was only one way to help him.

He didn't just needed to be defeated, he needed to be broken. So he could build himself back up.

1213222111111121111331121111111112111123.content:
Syrus LP: 1500
Dimitri LP: 5100

Syrus made his final draw, Limit Remover, and with that he knew that this duel was over. But first he need to make a few things very clear to Dimitri before he crushed his power fantasy.

"Do you want to know why you never win Dimitri?" Syrus asked calmly.

Dimitri felt a chill crawl up his spine when he heard Syrus's tone, but didn't let his unease break his persona. "I told you my name is-"

"Shut up."

Hearing that chilling tone again made the false king shut his yapper.

"The reason you never win is because you don't believe in your own skills." Syrus explained.

"What are you talking about?" Dimitri couldn't help but ask.

"Despite you clearly having talent, all you ever do is copy other duelists you believe are better then you. You mold yourself in their image as best you can, even copying their mannerisms and voices. But that's not who you really are. You're not Crowler, or Zane, or me, and you are most certainly not Yugi. By trying to be someone you're not, you fail to see your own potential. You fail to see what skills that only you are capable of. That's why you will never win with someone else's deck, using someone else's style, because it's not yours. And that why The Heart Of The Cards is not on your side. Because The Heart Of The Cards comes from believing in your own deck, your own skills, not someone's you stole or copied."

Hearing this wisdom really stuck a cord within Dimitri. But old habits die hard as the Life Points seem to tell him a different truth.

"A fine speech. But with how this duel seems to be in my favor, your words ring hollow." Dimitri stated with a confident smirk.

"You're right." Syrus admitted "If I really want to get through to you, I need to show you the true power of The Heart Of The Cards."

"What?!" the copycat exclaimed with worry.

"I summon Expressriod in Attack Mode!" the short champion shouted.

Expressriod ATK: 400

"And with Expressriod being summon to the field, I'm allowed to take 2 Machine-Type monsters from my graveyard and add them to my hand! And I choose to bring back the 2 I discard with Graceful Charity, Truckriod and Steathriod!"

And with that, the final pieces Syrus needed to bring forth his ultimate weapon were ready. Now it was time to bring them all together.

"And now, I play another one of my newest cards, Vehicroid Connection Zone!"

Hearing another card he knew wasn't in Syrus's deck before sent a wave of dread through Dimitri's body.

"This card allows me to special summon a Fusion monster from my deck so long as I discard the monsters I need to make the monster to the graveyard! Now, Drillriod and Expressriod from my field, Truckriod and Steathriod from my hand! Let's make some room for my deck's newest heavy hitter, Super Vehicriod - Stealth Union!!" Syrus bellowed passionately as his monster made it grand entrance.

And what an entrance it was. As like something you would see out of Power Rangers or Voltron, all the vehicriods combined together to form a giant mech that was ready to kick all kinds of ass.

Super Vehicriod - Stealth Union ATK: 3600

"Impossible?!" Dimitri shouted in fright at the sight of a monster more powerful then Black Luster Soldier.

"And my Stealth Union has a special ability that allows him to take any 1 monster on the field, so long as it isn't a Machine-Type, and equip them to it! Which then gives it access to that equipped monster's effects!"

"Any 1 monster?!" the false king exclaimed with horror.

"That's right! Including yours! And I choose to equip Stealth Union with Black Luster Soldier!" Syrus shouted with a smirk.

Steath Union's chest cavity opened to reveal a cockpit for the legendary knight to enter, which he did with a great leap and sat in the seat, leaving his sword and shield behind. But as the cockpit closed, the sword and shield grew until they became big enough for Steath Union to wield and flew into the mech's hands. And then in a flash of light, Stealth Union transformed into what can only be described as a giant, mechanized version of Black Luster Soldier.

"And now, say hello to Super Vehicriod - Black Luster Union!!" the young knight bellowed in triumph.

(Meanwhile with Syrus's friends)

"Woah.." was all Zane could say as he gazed in awe at the huge machine knight.

"That is the coolest thing I've ever seen." Jaden stated as a fact.

"Ditto.." Chumley whispered.

"Indeed.." Bastion agreed.

Alexis, Jazz and Mindy were barely able to restrain themselves from tackling their shared lover and making sweet love to him as they watched him summon the monster they all pitched in to buy as a Christmas present.

"We are SO rocking his world when this is over." Alexis stated strongly.

"Oh yeah.." her friends agreed.

(Back to the duel)

Dimitri was flabbergasted as he watched his opponent turn the monster that was suppose to insure his victory completely against him.

Meanwhile Syrus looked at the special abilities his mech now had access to, and his smirk widened "This works even better then I thought it would! Cause it says here that that 1 of Black Luster Soldiers effects is that when he destroys a monster in battle, the his allowed a second attack! And better still is that Stealth Union is also allowed a second attack when he has a monster equipped to him, at the cost of his ATK being reduced to half it's original ATK! This means that not only can he now attack 3 times, but his second attack won't decrease!"

"IT CAN'T BE!!!" the false king denied with sheer terror.

"Oh, but it can! But I would rather end this quickly, so I now activate Limit Remover!! This will double the ATK of all my Machince-Type monsters I control!! Now Black Luster Union, FINISH IT!!!" Syrus commanded.

Super Vehicroid - Black Luster Union ATK: 3600-7200

The Mechanical Knight did as commanded and slashed both spellcasters in half at the same time with his huge sword. Reducing them, and Dimitri's Life Points to nothing.

Dimitri LP: 5100-0

"And that's how you duel!!" the short champion exclaimed with a triumphant fist raised.

*Thunderous Applause*

"Huh?" Syrus looked behind to finally notice the large crowd that had gather to watch his epic duel. "How long of they been here?"

"Long enough to witness quite possibly the most spectacular duel this academy has ever seen!" Shepard announced his presence with his peers right behind as he approached the young duelist.

"Wait!! You mean!! Everyone saw the whole thing?!" Syrus asked with embarrassment of not noticing the crowd sooner.

"More or less." Shepard answered as he had eye full of pride for the young man "You have made dueling history tonight Syrus. Become one of the few duelists who have ever managed to defeat Yugi's Deck. Well done."

"You have done this academy and your dorm proud." Prof. Banner added with his trademark smile wider for his star pupil.

Fonda had a blush on her cheeks "Sir? Permission to reward Syrus for his stellar performance?" she asked her boss rather bashfully.

Shepard's smile widened a little "Granted."

The lovely nurse walked up to the short duelist and gave a sweet, but quick kiss on the cheek "Great work." she said softly with a radiant smile.

Even with all his new experience with the ladies, Syrus's face turned completely red and he nearly fainted in bliss like a dork.

"Yes yes congratulations, and all that." Crowler said impatiently as he kept his eye on Dimitri, who was on his knees shaking in despair form his defeat. "But now to deal with him."

"Yes. You are correct Dr." Shepard said gravely.

Syrus knew that Dimitri was more then likely going to be expelled for stealing, as it was the rules. And maybe worse since he did steal the most famous deck in world. And while Syrus was still very peeved about it, most of his fury was out of his system thanks to that intense duel. And it would be a shame if Dimitri was never allowed to duel again, cause if he could apply his skills to coming up with his own style, then he could become a truly fearsome opponent.

Maybe he should say something to convince Shepard to go easy on him?

"Chancellor Shepard!" Syrus called out to the headmaster.

"Yes?" he asked curiously.

"I know that what Dimitri did was a serious crime and he should be punished for it." Syrus laid out "That said, I think it would be better for him if he was also given the opportunity to redeem himself, rather then crush his dreams of ever becoming a great duelist. He has skill, he just needs better guidance."

Shepard blinked in surprise at the young man's show of mercy, but saw the logic in it. Besides, after the duel he just witnessed he was in a forgiving mood. "I see." he said thoughtfully as he approached the distraught duelist "Dimitri!" he call out to get his attention.

The copycat looked up with wide eyes full of fear "Y-yes sir?" he stammered in his real voice.

"For stealing Yugi's Deck you by all rights should be punished most severely. Not only for stealing the deck, trying to claim you are Yugi himself? It goes beyond identity theft, it's stolen valor! After all, Yugi is a national hero as well as great duelist!" the headmaster explained sternly "What you did is not only against campus rules, it's a major felony! Punishable by years in prison!"

Dimitri trembled in fright and shame as the full scope of what he did, as well as what was in store for him was made clear. "Oh god, what have I done?!"

"To be quite frank young man, you fucked up. Big time." Shepard said honestly "However, since I'm sure everyone here can agree that seeing Yugi's Deck in action is much better then just seeing it on display. And all things considered, you wielded the deck quite well. Almost like Yugi himself, and that is an impressive feat. Plus, I can tell you are truly sorry for what you did. And for that your punishment will be reduced."

"It will?!" Dimitri asked in shock.

"Yes. Instead of being expelled and sent to prison, you will be suspended for the rest of the year. When you return, you will repeat your first year as a Slifer. And you will be spending most of that year in detention with Dr. Crowler, who will no doubt have plenty of make up work and extra credit for you to do."

"Oh most certainly Chancellor." Crowler said with glee at the thought of being able to punish the thieving scum himself.

"Now, go pack your things. You leave for home first thing in the morning." Shepard instructed.

"Y-yes sir!" Dimitri complied heading off to his dorm room.

"The Deck!" Shepard reminded as he held out his hand.

"O-oh right! I-I'm so sorry!" Dimitri stammered awkwardly as he placed the deck in the headmaster's hand.

"Hey Dimitri!" Syrus called out.

Dimitri looked to see rather a glare of righteous fury from his former opponent, he saw a friendly smile.

"When you get back next year, I'd love to duel you again!" Syrus said sincerely "But next time, do it with a deck you made yourself!"

The former copycat didn't know what to say to such kindness he believe was undeserved for him. But all he could say was with the upmost humility "Thank you."

Syrus just gave a nod as he watched the humbled young man leave, glad to know that he was taking the first step towards bettering himself. But then, he felt 3 pairs of familiar feminine arms wrap around him and lift him in the air. "What are-?"

"Bedroom. Now." his lovers stated with no room for argument.

Seeing their friend getting kidnapped, Jaden and Chumley looked to Bastion "Hey, can we crash at your place for the night?" Jadem pleaded.

The Ra genius sighed.
